/cwe/weakness/64.Name: Windows Shortcut Following (.LNK)
/cwe/weakness/64.Description: The product, when opening a file or directory, does not sufficiently handle when the file is a Windows shortcut (.LNK) whose target is outside of the intended control sphere. This could allow an attacker to cause the product to operate on unauthorized files.
/cwe/weakness/64.ExtendedDescription: The shortcut (file with the .lnk extension) can permit an attacker to read/write a file that they originally did not have permissions to access.

PotentialMitigations.Description:
Follow the principle of least privilege when assigning access rights to entities in a software system.


Denying access to a file can prevent an attacker from replacing that file with a link to a sensitive file. Ensure good compartmentalization in the system to provide protected areas that can be trusted.
